summary: Fair‑quality audience recording of a memorable late‑era show highlighted by an epic Days Between and a stellar New Speedway > Nobody's Fault sequence
review: Fans rave about the musical highs – a soaring Days Between, a blazing New Speedway into Nobody's Fault, and inspired renditions of Stagger Lee, Deal and Miracle – while noting a few rough spots (sloppy first set, post‑space lull and a weak Lovelight closer). The audience‑sourced tape is generally listenable but suffers from audible chatter during the NSB/NFB segment.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance (average 4‑5★) but several call out recording noise and occasional sloppiness, pulling the overall weighted score to a solid mid‑range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Background chatter audible during New Speedway > Nobody's Fault; otherwise decent clarity and listenable audio, though not as clean as a soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but a bit uneven; the early half of the second set flows beautifully (NSB > NFB, Saint opener, Miracle), then loses momentum after the post‑space segment before ending on an emotional Brokedown encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for emotive vocals on Saint of Circumstance and a heartfelt solo on Days Between; his bluesy lead on New Speedway is called 'great' despite slight sloppiness, though one reviewer notes he was beginning to drift out of it.
band_member_comments.Phil: No direct comments on bass playing, but Lesh’s quote calling Days Between "achingly nostalgic" underscores his contribution to the song's emotional weight.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers provide an extended ~10‑minute jam before Miracle and receive a neutral nod for the Women Are Smarter segue; overall rhythm section is described as solid.
